title: -Adjustment to Police Accountability Board Stipend Cap Following AAO #1 Carryover Approval

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a resolution temporarily increasing the stipend cap for Police Accountability Board
(PAB) members to ensure the distribution of previously approved funds and provide
compensation for meetings that exceeded the existing monthly cap.
SUMMARY
PAB members currently receive stipends of $110 per meeting, up to four meetings per
month, as established by Resolution No. 69,739-N.S., adopted on March 9, 2021. On
November 19, 2024, the City Council allocated $12,000 in FY 2024 carryover funds for
“PAB Committee + Training Stipends Adjustments” as part of the Annual Appropriations
Ordinance #1 (AAO#1).1 These funds were intended to compensate PAB members for
their expanded workload in 2024, including increased participation in subcommittees and
training sessions. A temporary adjustment to the stipend cap is necessary to equitably
distribute these funds in accordance with the Berkeley City Charter. While the ODPA
secured additional stipend funding in the amount of $12,000 through AAO#1,
implementation of a stipend increase requires City Council action via resolution, per
Berkeley City Charter Section 125(11)(b).2
sections.current_situation:
Berkeley City Charter Section 125(11)(b) authorizes the City Council to adjust Board
member stipends no more than once per fiscal year, and not in excess of the Bay Area
cost-of-living increase as measured by official U.S. economic reports. Berkeley City
Charter Section(11)(b) states:
Adjustments to Board member stipends shall occur no more than
once in a fiscal year and in no event shall an increase in Board

The City Clerk clarified in a March 7, 2025 correspondence that any increase in the
stipend cap requires Council action through a submitted report and resolution:
To set the new rates, the ODPA can submit an item to Council
showing the authority and calculations for the new amounts. The
increase should be adopted in a Council Resolution that you include
with the report.
This step is necessary even though the Council approved the associated funding via the
AAO#1 carryover process.
sections.background:
The current stipend structure was set in Resolution No. 64,831-N.S, which established a
per-meeting stipend of $100 and authorized CPI-based annual increases.3 This structure
applies to the Police Accountability Board and a small number of other bodies.
The Board’s 2024 Workplan included oversight responsibilities and initiatives requiring
time-intensive engagement beyond regular meetings, including:
 Implementation of an Early Intervention System (EIS)
 Finalization of the PAB’s and ODPA’s Regulations for Handling Investigations and
Complaints4
 Extensive review of operational manuals and policies, including updates to the
BPD’s Vehicle Pursuit Policy, Body-Worn Camera Policy, and other key policies.
To reflect the sustained increase in workload experienced during FY 2024, the ODPA
successfully secured $12,000 in carryover funds under AAO#1 to support increased
stipend costs. These funds, combined with existing budget authority, will enable the City
to equitably compensate Board members once a new resolution is adopted.
3 March 9, 2021 Referral Response: Commission Low-Income Stipend Reform:
https://berkeleyca.gov/sites/default/files/documents/2021-03-
09%20Item%2003%20Referral%20Response%20Commission%20Low-Income.pdf
4 Berkeley Charter Section 125(18)(a) requires that the Director of Police Accountability and Board adopt
regulations for handling complaints filed with the Director of Police Accountability by any member of the
public alleging misconduct by sworn employees of the Police Department and undertake investigations of
complaints as they deem warranted. The regulations shall include the following: (1) What constitutes a
complaint; and (2) A provision for voluntary mediation of complaints in lieu of an investigation.
Since the implementation of Measure II, the PAB and ODPA have been handling complaints through the
Interim Regulations for Handling Complaints Against Sworn Officers of the Police Department Approved by
the City Council October 5, 2021.

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###–N.S.
AUTHORIZING A TEMPORARY ADJUSTMENT TO THE POLICE
ACCOUNTABILITY BOARD STIPEND CAP FOR FISCAL YEAR 2025 PURSUANT TO
BERKELEY CITY CHARTER SECTION 125(11)(b)
WHEREAS, Berkeley City Charter Article XVIII, Section 125(11)(b) authorizes the City Council
to adjust the stipends paid to Police Accountability Board (PAB) members no more than once in a
fiscal year and not in excess of the annual change in the cost of living for the San Francisco Bay
Area, as measured by official United States economic reports; and
WHEREAS, Resolution No. 69,739–N.S., adopted on March 9, 2021, established a stipend of
$100 per meeting for PAB members, not to exceed four meetings per month, with future
adjustments tied to the Consumer Price Index (CPI); and
WHEREAS, on November 19, 2024, the City Council approved Resolution 7,940 N.S. which
authorized $12,000 in carryover funding through the FY 2024 Annual Appropriations Ordinance
#1 (AAO#1) to fund additional compensation for increased participation by PAB members in
subcommittee meetings and training during Fiscal Year 2025; and
WHEREAS, the Board’s 2024 Workplan included oversight responsibilities and initiatives that
required time-intensive engagement beyond regular meetings, including the implementation of an
Early Intervention System (EIS), the finalization of the PAB’s and ODPA’s Regulations for
Handling Investigations and Complaints, and an extensive review of operational manuals and
policies, such as updates to the BPD’s Vehicle Pursuit Policy, Body-Worn Camera Policy, and
other key policies.
WHEREAS, the Office of the Director of Police Accountability confirms that sufficient
appropriations are available in
